phase_description: Phase 3 second post-review defect-fix cycle. All v0.3.1 content plus: B1 — v5r-i compound verbs emit correct nai_form with stem prefix (でない/ことがない/でもない) and blank malformed potential/passive/imperative/volitional/causative/conditional_ba on compound entries; B2 — docs/gaps.md jinmeiyō entry marked ADDRESSED; B3 — docs/sources.md kanjidic2-en row updated to kanjidic2-all; M2 — schemas/stroke-order.schema.json documents warnings and stroke_count_mismatches metadata fields (schemaVersion 0.3.2); M3 — stats.py prints both TOTAL (all rows) and UNIQUE COMMITTED; CHANGELOG [0.3.0] rows/unique split; M4 — four data integrity invariant tests added (jlpt_waller enum, sentence-id roundtrip, grammar-id roundtrip, kanji/word cross-ref inverse); B1 regression tests. Also ships the previously-unreleased jinmeiyō view (863 entries), Tatoeba linkage for grammar examples, CI smoke test workflow, and stroke-count-mismatch metadata. 54/54 tests passing. Phase 4 remains pending.

jmdict-examples-eng.description: JMdict English entries with Tatoeba example sentences pre-linked.

kanjivg.description: KanjiVG stroke order SVG files (non-variant forms).

kanjium-accents.description: Kanjium pitch accent TSV (word, reading, mora positions).

kanjidic2.description: KANJIDIC2 kanji data, all languages (13,108 characters, full coverage).

waller-jlpt-vocab-n5.description: Jonathan Waller JLPT N5 vocabulary list (CSV) via stephenmk/yomitan-jlpt-vocab.

waller-jlpt-vocab-n4.description: Jonathan Waller JLPT N4 vocabulary list (CSV) via stephenmk/yomitan-jlpt-vocab.

waller-jlpt-vocab-n3.description: Jonathan Waller JLPT N3 vocabulary list (CSV) via stephenmk/yomitan-jlpt-vocab.

waller-jlpt-vocab-n2.description: Jonathan Waller JLPT N2 vocabulary list (CSV) via stephenmk/yomitan-jlpt-vocab.

waller-jlpt-vocab-n1.description: Jonathan Waller JLPT N1 vocabulary list (CSV) via stephenmk/yomitan-jlpt-vocab.

waller-jlpt-kanji.description: Jonathan Waller JLPT kanji classifications via davidluzgouveia/kanji-data (jlpt_new field only). Code is MIT; data fields derive from Waller CC-BY.

next_actions: Grammar v0.3.x patches: expand coverage beyond N5+N4 selections. Target N3 grammar points in v0.3.1, N2 in v0.3.2, N1 in v0.3.3.
Recruit native-speaker reviewers to move grammar entries from draft → native_speaker_reviewed (see docs/contributing.md).
Text-match Tatoeba sentences against grammar-curated examples to populate Tatoeba sentence IDs where exact matches exist.

Phase 4: evaluate candidates in docs/phase4-candidates.md — notably JPDB modern frequency (blocked on license clarification), radical meanings/Kangxi numbers, classical handwriting datasets, and audio corpora.
